refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these functions are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are stored or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Map worksite mission context inputs to performance requirement parameters
Generate and document performance targets based on mapped parameters
Maintain a versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates
Validate performance targets against safety plans and operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Map worksite mission context inputs to performance requirement parameters

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the input mapping from documentation and history maintenance, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that changes in safety plans or operational policies may require updates to mapping logic
Assumes versioning and documentation mechanisms are handled in separate DPs or IMs
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating documentation and history maintenance DPs to ensure clear modular boundaries and traceability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: This module encompasses the overall system logic for mapping worksite mission context inputs to performance requirement parameters, integrating data from various sources and producing parameter outputs for downstream modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context inputs and sensor data relevant to the worksite.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends mapped performance requirement parameters to tasking and control modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems for updated mission context and parameter feedback.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it centralizes the mapping logic; minimal diffusion as inputs and outputs are well defined within this system-level module.
Partial containment possible but mapping logic would be spread across vehicle control and sensor processing, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and would require significant diffusion to contain mapping logic.
Safety Services are specialized and not suited to contain mission context mapping, leading to high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP because it is the system-level module designed to integrate mission context inputs and produce performance parameters, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution if too many responsibilities are centralized.
Interface definitions may evolve, requiring updates to external interfaces and module boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks grow, consider decomposing NGHE Solution into submodules that separately handle context input processing and parameter mapping, maintaining clear interfaces without leaking implementation details.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically and directly addresses the FR by specifying the mapping of worksite mission context inputs to performance requirement parameters, fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from mission context.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as the DP is contained within the NGHE Solution module with a diffusion score of 2, indicating minimal scattering across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module centralizes the mapping logic with well-defined interfaces, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion. Other modules show higher diffusion scores and lower containment suitability.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with telemetry inputs and outputs performance parameters, allowing traceability and documentation of performance targets as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module with defined external interfaces supports straightforward verification and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module; if it becomes too complex, decompose into submodules handling context input processing and parameter mapping separately.
Maintain and update interface definitions proactively to accommodate evolving mission context inputs and parameter feedback.
Ensure documentation rigor to maintain traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and history of baseline/calibration updates.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and low diffusion risk. The NGHE Solution module centralizes the DP implementation with clear interfaces and feasible verification, ensuring high cohesion and traceability. Although the system is safety critical, the current decomposition level sufficiently addresses complexity and containment. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring complexity growth and maintaining documentation rather than further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module and consider decomposing into submodules if complexity increases, to maintain clarity and manageability.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain low diffusion and high cohesion as system evolves.
Verification processes will be maintained to ensure traceability and ownership.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module that may require future decomposition.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring reports on module complexity and diffusion metrics.
Verification documentation linking performance targets to mission context inputs.
